_comment: Publisher-specific metadata quirks and formatting notes for IEEE-style citations

IEEE.note: Standard IEEE formatting — use vol., no., pp., doi directly

ACM.note: ACM uses Article Number instead of page ranges for some publications

Elsevier.note: Elsevier journals use standard vol/issue/page format. Some use article numbers.

Springer.note: Springer LNCS uses volume for book-series numbering. Conference papers in LNCS: treat as book chapter.
Springer.lncs_format: in Lecture Notes Comput. Sci., vol. {volume}, pp. {start}–{end}

Wiley.note: Wiley uses standard format. Some journals use early-access article numbers.

MDPI.note: MDPI open-access journals often use article numbers instead of page ranges.

IET.note: IET journals follow similar format to IEEE. Use abbreviated journal names.

Nature Publishing Group.note: Nature journals: abbreviated name, vol, pages, year.

AAAS.note: Science journal. Standard format: vol, issue, pages.

AIP.note: American Institute of Physics — J. Appl. Phys., Appl. Phys. Lett., etc.

APS.note: American Physical Society — Phys. Rev. Lett., Phys. Rev. B, etc.

SAE.note: SAE Technical Papers use paper number as identifier.

arXiv.note: Preprints — format as: Author, "Title," arXiv preprint arXiv:{id}, Year.
